revision.text:

== 主な天皇神社 == * [[天皇神社 (焼津市)|天皇神社]] – [[静岡県]][[焼津市]]駅北 * [[天皇神社 (大津市)|天皇神社]] – [[滋賀県]][[大津市]]和邇中 * [[天皇神社 (天理市)|天皇神社]] – [[京都府]][[亀岡市]]大井町 * [[天皇神社 (天理市)|天皇神社]] – [[奈良県]][[天理市]]備前町 ： 旧村社
== 関連項目 ==

{{Shrine | Name = Tenno Shrine | image = [[File:Tenno shrine (Otsu, Shiga).jpg|300px]] | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Otsu City|ja|大津市}} Shiga Prefecture Otsu City Wani‐naka 146 | Coordinates = {{WikiCoord2|35|09|21.689|N|135|54|58.078|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚尊}} | Sacred Object = | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = | Votive Boards, etc. = | Annual Festival = Second Sunday in May | Rituals = | Establishment = }}
'''Tenno Shrine''' (天皇神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Otsu City|ja|大津市}}.
== Geography == {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|lt_ja=JR}}{{ill|Wani Station|ja|和邇駅}} is located approximately 1.1 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル}} to the southwest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/158|title=天皇神社{{!}}滋賀県観光情報|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわこビジターズビューロー|date=2015-06-23}}</ref>, and a 10‐minute walk away <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nishioumi.ct-net.com/00-05.shtml|title=歴史散歩・和邇天皇神社 和邇祭り|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=京都新聞西近江販売所連合|year=2000|month=05}}</ref>. It is situated along the roadside of Ryūka Crossing (present‐day {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|ja|滋賀県道311号途中志賀線}}) {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. It is about 5 minutes from {{ill|Kosai Road|ja|湖西道路}} ({{ill|National Route 161|ja|国道161号}}) {{ill|Wani IC|ja|和迩インターチェンジ|lt=Wani IC|lt_ja=和邇IC}} <ref name=":2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://otsu.or.jp/thingstodo/spot213|title=天皇神社|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわ湖大津観光協会}}</ref>.
== History == In 966 ({{ill|Kōhō|ja|康保}} 3) the Gozu Tennō of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} was returned, and it is said that it began under the name "Wani Gozu Tennōsha" {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|lt=Kamakura|lt_ja=鎌倉}} <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|1324|ja|1324}} (the first year of {{ill|Shōchū|ja|正中 (元号)|lt=Shōchū|lt_ja=正中}}) and has been designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571年)|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち}}, when the portable shrine of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historic Heritage of Shiga Along the Highways|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefecture Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division}}== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shrines in Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県の神社一覧}} * {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}

{{ill|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)|ja|天皇神社 (大津市)}} {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天皇神社}} [[category:kk]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/天皇神社]] [[Category:11]] {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)|ja|天皇神社 (大津市)}} {{ill|Tenno Shrine|en|Tenno Shrine}} [[Category:translated shrines and temples]] <!--Tenno Shrine (Otsu)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Tenno Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)|en|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)|en|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)|en|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:11]]
{{Shrine | Name = Tenno Shrine | image = [[File:Tenno shrine (Otsu, Shiga).jpg|300px]] | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Otsu City|ja|大津市}} Shiga Prefecture Otsu City Wani‐naka 146 | Coordinates = {{WikiCoord2|35|09|21.689|N|135|54|58.078|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚尊}} | Sacred Object = | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = | Votive Boards, etc. = | Annual Festival = Second Sunday in May | Rituals = | Establishment = }}
'''Tenno Shrine''' (天皇神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Otsu City|ja|大津市}}.
== Geography == {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|lt_ja=JR}}{{ill|Wani Station|ja|和邇駅}} is located approximately 1.1 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル}} to the southwest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/158|title=天皇神社{{!}}滋賀県観光情報|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわこビジターズビューロー|date=2015-06-23}}</ref>, and a 10‐minute walk away <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nishioumi.ct-net.com/00-05.shtml|title=歴史散歩・和邇天皇神社 和邇祭り|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=京都新聞西近江販売所連合|year=2000|month=05}}</ref>. It is situated along the roadside of Ryūka Crossing (present‐day {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|ja|滋賀県道311号途中志賀線}}) {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. It is about 5 minutes from {{ill|Kosai Road|ja|湖西道路}} ({{ill|National Route 161|ja|国道161号}}) {{ill|Wani IC|ja|和迩インターチェンジ|lt=Wani IC|lt_ja=和邇IC}} <ref name=":2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://otsu.or.jp/thingstodo/spot213|title=天皇神社|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわ湖大津観光協会}}</ref>.
== History == In 966 ({{ill|Kōhō|ja|康保}} 3) the Gozu Tennō of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} was returned, and it is said that it began under the name "Wani Gozu Tennōsha" {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|lt=Kamakura|lt_ja=鎌倉}} <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|1324|ja|1324}} (the first year of {{ill|Shōchū|ja|正中 (元号)|lt=Shōchū|lt_ja=正中}}) and has been designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571年)|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち}}, when the portable shrine of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historic Heritage of Shiga Along the Highways|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefecture Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shrines in Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県の神社一覧}} * {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}

[[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenno Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenno Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenno Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:天皇神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{ill|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)|ja|天皇神社 (大津市)}} {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天皇神社}} [[Category:kk]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/天皇神社]] [[Category:11]] {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)|ja|天皇神社 (大津市)}} {{ill|Tenno Shrine|en|Tenno Shrine}} [[Category:translated shrines and temples]] <!--Tenno Shrine (Otsu)-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Tenno Shrine (Otsu)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)|en|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)|en|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] {{ill|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)|en|Tenno Shrine (Otsu)}} [[Category:11]]
{{Shrine | Name = Tenno Shrine | image = [[File:Tenno shrine (Otsu, Shiga).jpg|300px]] | Location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Otsu City|ja|大津市}} Shiga Prefecture Otsu City Wani‐naka 146 | Coordinates = {{WikiCoord2|35|09|21.689|N|135|54|58.078|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=素戔嗚尊}} | Sacred Object = | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = | Votive Boards, etc. = | Annual Festival = Second Sunday in May | Rituals = | Establishment = }}
'''Tenno Shrine''' (天皇神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Otsu City|ja|大津市}}.
== Geography == {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|lt=JR|lt_ja=JR}}{{ill|Wani Station|ja|和邇駅}} is located approximately 1.1 {{ill|kilometer|ja|キロメートル}} to the southwest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/158|title=天皇神社{{!}}滋賀県観光情報|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわこビジターズビューロー|date=2015-06-23}}</ref>, and a 10‐minute walk away <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nishioumi.ct-net.com/00-05.shtml|title=歴史散歩・和邇天皇神社 和邇祭り|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=京都新聞西近江販売所連合|year=2000|month=05}}</ref>. It is situated along the roadside of Ryūka Crossing (present‐day {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|ja|滋賀県道311号途中志賀線}}) {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. It is about 5 minutes from {{ill|Kosai Road|ja|湖西道路}} ({{ill|National Route 161|ja|国道161号}}) {{ill|Wani IC|ja|和迩インターチェンジ|lt=Wani IC|lt_ja=和邇IC}} <ref name=":2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://otsu.or.jp/thingstodo/spot213|title=天皇神社|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわ湖大津観光協会}}</ref>.
== History == In 966 ({{ill|Kōhō|ja|康保}} 3) the Gozu Tennō of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} was returned, and it is said that it began under the name "Wani Gozu Tennōsha" {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|lt=Kamakura|lt_ja=鎌倉}} <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|1324|ja|1324}} (the first year of {{ill|Shōchū|ja|正中 (元号)|lt=Shōchū|lt_ja=正中}}) and has been designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}}'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち}}, when the portable shrine of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historic Heritage of Shiga Along the Highways|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefecture Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shrines in Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県の神社一覧}} * {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社}}

'''Tenno Shrine''' (天皇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]].
== Geography == [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Wani Station|Wani Station]] is located approximately 1.1 [[:en:Kilometre|kilometer]] to the southwest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/158|title=天皇神社{{!}}滋賀県観光情報|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわこビジターズビューロー|date=2015-06-23}}</ref>, and a 10‐minute walk away <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nishioumi.ct-net.com/00-05.shtml|title=歴史散歩・和邇天皇神社 和邇祭り|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=京都新聞西近江販売所連合|year=2000|month=05}}</ref>. It is situated along the roadside of Ryūka Crossing (present‐day {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|ja|滋賀県道311号途中志賀線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|qq=|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 311|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564859}}) {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. It is about 5 minutes from {{ill|Kosai Road|ja|湖西道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosai Road|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 琵琶湖西縦貫道路|1=Lake Biwa west running through road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573837}} ([[:en:Japan National Route 161|National Route 161]]) {{ill|Wani IC|ja|和迩インターチェンジ|lt=Wani IC|lt_ja=和邇IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani IC|qq=|1=Wani interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418218}} <ref name=":2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://otsu.or.jp/thingstodo/spot213|title=天皇神社|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわ湖大津観光協会}}</ref>.
== History == In 966 ([[:en:Kōhō|Kōhō]] 3) the Gozu Tennō of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was returned, and it is said that it began under the name "Wani Gozu Tennōsha" {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura]] <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:1324|1324]] (the first year of [[:en:Shōchū (era)|Shōchū]]) and has been designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gabled Roof Construction|qq=|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Takamura Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Dōfū Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toad‐leg Motif|qq=|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|qq=|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|qq=|1=Minamihama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|qq=|1=Nakahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|qq=|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|qq=|1=Imaju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|qq=|1=Takashiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天皇神社 (大津市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenno Shrine | image = [[File:Tenno shrine (Otsu, Shiga).jpg|300px]] | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]] Shiga Prefecture Otsu City Wani‐naka 146 | Coordinates = {{WikiCoord2|35|09|21.689|N|135|54|58.078|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] | Sacred Object = | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = | Votive Boards, etc. = | Annual Festival = Second Sunday in May | Rituals = | Establishment = }}
'''Tenno Shrine''' (天皇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]].
== Geography == [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Wani Station|Wani Station]] is located approximately 1.1 [[:en:Kilometre|kilometer]] to the southwest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/158|title=天皇神社{{!}}滋賀県観光情報|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわこビジターズビューロー|date=2015-06-23}}</ref>, and a 10‐minute walk away <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nishioumi.ct-net.com/00-05.shtml|title=歴史散歩・和邇天皇神社 和邇祭り|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=京都新聞西近江販売所連合|year=2000|month=05}}</ref>. It is situated along the roadside of Ryūka Crossing (present‐day {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|ja|滋賀県道311号途中志賀線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 311|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564859}}) {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. It is about 5 minutes from {{ill|Kosai Road|ja|湖西道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosai Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 琵琶湖西縦貫道路|1=Lake Biwa west running through road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573837}} ([[:en:Japan National Route 161|National Route 161]]) {{ill|Wani IC|ja|和迩インターチェンジ|lt=Wani IC|lt_ja=和邇IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani IC|1=Wani interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418218}} <ref name=":2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://otsu.or.jp/thingstodo/spot213|title=天皇神社|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわ湖大津観光協会}}</ref>.
== History == In 966 ([[:en:Kōhō|Kōhō]] 3) the Gozu Tennō of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was returned, and it is said that it began under the name "Wani Gozu Tennōsha" {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura]] <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:1324|1324]] (the first year of [[:en:Shōchū (era)|Shōchū]]) and has been designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gabled Roof Construction|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Takamura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toad‐leg Motif|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|1=Minamihama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|1=Nakahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|1=Imaju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|1=Takashiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天皇神社 (大津市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenno Shrine | image = [[File:Tenno shrine (Otsu, Shiga).jpg|300px]] | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]] Shiga Prefecture Otsu City Wani‐naka 146 | Coordinates = {{WikiCoord2|35|09|21.689|N|135|54|58.078|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] | Sacred Object = | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = | Votive Boards, etc. = | Annual Festival = Second Sunday in May | Rituals = | Establishment = }}
'''Tenno Shrine''' (天皇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]].
== Geography == [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Wani Station|Wani Station]] is located approximately 1.1 [[:en:Kilometre|kilometer]] to the southwest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/158|title=天皇神社{{!}}滋賀県観光情報|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわこビジターズビューロー|date=2015-06-23}}</ref>, and a 10‐minute walk away <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nishioumi.ct-net.com/00-05.shtml|title=歴史散歩・和邇天皇神社 和邇祭り|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=京都新聞西近江販売所連合|year=2000|month=05}}</ref>. It is situated along the roadside of Ryūka Crossing (present‐day {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|ja|滋賀県道311号途中志賀線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 311|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564859}}) {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. It is about 5 minutes from {{ill|Kosai Road|ja|湖西道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosai Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 琵琶湖西縦貫道路|1=Lake Biwa west running through road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573837}} ([[:en:Japan National Route 161|National Route 161]]) {{ill|Wani IC|ja|和迩インターチェンジ|lt=Wani IC|lt_ja=和邇IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani IC|1=Wani interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418218}} <ref name=":2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://otsu.or.jp/thingstodo/spot213|title=天皇神社|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわ湖大津観光協会}}</ref>.
== History == In 966 ([[:en:Kōhō|Kōhō]] 3) the Gozu Tennō of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was returned, and it is said that it began under the name "Wani Gozu Tennōsha" {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura]] <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:1324|1324]] (the first year of [[:en:Shōchū (era)|Shōchū]]) and has been designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gabled Roof Construction|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Takamura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toad‐leg Motif|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|1=Minamihama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|1=Nakahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|1=Imaju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|1=Takashiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historic Heritage of Shiga Along the Highways|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefecture Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shrines in Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県の神社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines in Shiga Prefecture|1=List of Shrines in Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564591}} * {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|天皇神社|version=103665843|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Completion tier 1]] }}
[[ja:天皇神社]]

== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|1=Minamihama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|1=Nakahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|1=Imaju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|1=Takashiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天皇神社 (大津市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenno Shrine | image = [[File:Tenno shrine (Otsu, Shiga).jpg|300px]] | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]] Shiga Prefecture Otsu City Wani‐naka 146 | Coordinates = {{WikiCoord2|35|09|21.689|N|135|54|58.078|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] | Sacred Object = | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = | Votive Boards, etc. = | Annual Festival = Second Sunday in May | Rituals = | Establishment = }}
'''Tenno Shrine''' (天皇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]].
== Geography == [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Wani Station|Wani Station]] is located approximately 1.1 [[:en:Kilometre|kilometer]] to the southwest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/158|title=天皇神社{{!}}滋賀県観光情報|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわこビジターズビューロー|date=2015-06-23}}</ref>, and a 10‐minute walk away <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nishioumi.ct-net.com/00-05.shtml|title=歴史散歩・和邇天皇神社 和邇祭り|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=京都新聞西近江販売所連合|year=2000|month=05}}</ref>. It is situated along the roadside of Ryūka Crossing (present‐day {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|ja|滋賀県道311号途中志賀線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 311|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564859}}) {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. It is about 5 minutes from {{ill|Kosai Road|ja|湖西道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosai Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 琵琶湖西縦貫道路|1=Lake Biwa west running through road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573837}} ([[:en:Japan National Route 161|National Route 161]]) {{ill|Wani IC|ja|和迩インターチェンジ|lt=Wani IC|lt_ja=和邇IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani IC|1=Wani interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418218}} <ref name=":2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://otsu.or.jp/thingstodo/spot213|title=天皇神社|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわ湖大津観光協会}}</ref>.
== History == In 966 ([[:en:Kōhō|Kōhō]] 3) the Gozu Tennō of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was returned, and it is said that it began under the name "Wani Gozu Tennōsha" {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura]] <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:1324|1324]] (the first year of [[:en:Shōchū (era)|Shōchū]]) and has been designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gabled Roof Construction|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Takamura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toad‐leg Motif|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|1=Minamihama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|1=Nakahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|1=Imaju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|1=Takashiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.

== Geography == [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Wani Station|Wani Station]] is located approximately 1.1 [[:en:Kilometre|kilometer]] to the southwest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/158|title=天皇神社{{!}}滋賀県観光情報|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわこビジターズビューロー|date=2015-06-23}}</ref>, and a 10‐minute walk away <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nishioumi.ct-net.com/00-05.shtml|title=歴史散歩・和邇天皇神社 和邇祭り|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=京都新聞西近江販売所連合|year=2000|month=05}}</ref>. It is situated along the roadside of Ryūka Crossing (present‐day {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|ja|滋賀県道311号途中志賀線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 311|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564859}}) {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. It is about 5 minutes from {{ill|Kosai Road|ja|湖西道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosai Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 琵琶湖西縦貫道路|1=Lake Biwa west running through road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573837}} ([[:en:Japan National Route 161|National Route 161]]) {{ill|Wani IC|ja|和迩インターチェンジ|lt=Wani IC|lt_ja=和邇IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani IC|1=Wani interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418218}} <ref name=":2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://otsu.or.jp/thingstodo/spot213|title=天皇神社|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわ湖大津観光協会}}</ref>.
== History == In 966 ([[:en:Kōhō|Kōhō]] 3) the Gozu Tennō of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was returned, and it is said that it began under the name "Wani Gozu Tennōsha" {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura]] <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:1324|1324]] (the first year of [[:en:Shōchū (era)|Shōchū]]) and has been designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gabled Roof Construction|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Takamura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toad‐leg Motif|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|1=Minamihama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|1=Nakahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|1=Imaju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|1=Takashiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.

The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|1=Minamihama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|1=Nakahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|1=Imaju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|1=Takashiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天皇神社 (大津市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenno Shrine | image = [[File:Tenno shrine (Otsu, Shiga).jpg|300px]] | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]] Shiga Prefecture Otsu City Wani‐naka 146 | Coordinates = {{WikiCoord2|35|09|21.689|N|135|54|58.078|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] | Sacred Object = | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = | Votive Boards, etc. = | Annual Festival = Second Sunday in May | Rituals = | Establishment = }}
'''Tenno Shrine''' (天皇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]].
== Geography == [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Wani Station|Wani Station]] is located approximately 1.1 [[:en:Kilometre|kilometer]] to the southwest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/158|title=天皇神社{{!}}滋賀県観光情報|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわこビジターズビューロー|date=2015-06-23}}</ref>, and a 10‐minute walk away <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nishioumi.ct-net.com/00-05.shtml|title=歴史散歩・和邇天皇神社 和邇祭り|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=京都新聞西近江販売所連合|year=2000|month=05}}</ref>. It is situated along the roadside of Ryūka Crossing (present‐day {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|ja|滋賀県道311号途中志賀線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 311|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564859}}) {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. It is about 5 minutes from {{ill|Kosai Road|ja|湖西道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosai Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 琵琶湖西縦貫道路|1=Lake Biwa west running through road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573837}} ([[:en:Japan National Route 161|National Route 161]]) {{ill|Wani IC|ja|和迩インターチェンジ|lt=Wani IC|lt_ja=和邇IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani IC|1=Wani interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418218}} <ref name=":2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://otsu.or.jp/thingstodo/spot213|title=天皇神社|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわ湖大津観光協会}}</ref>.
== History == In 966 ([[:en:Kōhō|Kōhō]] 3) the Gozu Tennō of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was returned, and it is said that it began under the name "Wani Gozu Tennōsha" {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura]] <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:1324|1324]] (the first year of [[:en:Shōchū (era)|Shōchū]]) and has been designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gabled Roof Construction|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Takamura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toad‐leg Motif|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|1=Minamihama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|1=Nakahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|1=Imaju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|1=Takashiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天皇神社 (大津市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenno Shrine | image = [[File:Tenno shrine (Otsu, Shiga).jpg|300px]] | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]] Shiga Prefecture Otsu City Wani‐naka 146 | Coordinates = {{WikiCoord2|35|09|21.689|N|135|54|58.078|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] | Sacred Object = | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = | Votive Boards, etc. = | Annual Festival = Second Sunday in May | Rituals = | Establishment = }}
'''Tenno Shrine''' (天皇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]].
== Geography == [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Wani Station|Wani Station]] is located approximately 1.1 [[:en:Kilometre|kilometer]] to the southwest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/158|title=天皇神社{{!}}滋賀県観光情報|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわこビジターズビューロー|date=2015-06-23}}</ref>, and a 10‐minute walk away <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nishioumi.ct-net.com/00-05.shtml|title=歴史散歩・和邇天皇神社 和邇祭り|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=京都新聞西近江販売所連合|year=2000|month=05}}</ref>. It is situated along the roadside of Ryūka Crossing (present‐day {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|ja|滋賀県道311号途中志賀線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 311|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564859}}) {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. It is about 5 minutes from {{ill|Kosai Road|ja|湖西道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosai Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 琵琶湖西縦貫道路|1=Lake Biwa west running through road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573837}} ([[:en:Japan National Route 161|National Route 161]]) {{ill|Wani IC|ja|和迩インターチェンジ|lt=Wani IC|lt_ja=和邇IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani IC|1=Wani interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418218}} <ref name=":2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://otsu.or.jp/thingstodo/spot213|title=天皇神社|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわ湖大津観光協会}}</ref>.
== History == In 966 ([[:en:Kōhō|Kōhō]] 3) the Gozu Tennō of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was returned, and it is said that it began under the name "Wani Gozu Tennōsha" {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura]] <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:1324|1324]] (the first year of [[:en:Shōchū (era)|Shōchū]]) and has been designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gabled Roof Construction|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Takamura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toad‐leg Motif|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|1=Minamihama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|1=Nakahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|1=Imaju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|1=Takashiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Historic Heritage of Shiga Along the Highways|date=2019-10-25|year=2019|publisher=Sunrise Publishing|ref=harv|author=Shiga Prefecture Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shrines in Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県の神社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines in Shiga Prefecture|1=List of Shrines in Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564591}} * {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine|qq=}}

The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|1=Minamihama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|1=Nakahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|1=Imaju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|1=Takashiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天皇神社 (大津市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天皇神社 (大津市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenno Shrine | image = [[File:Tenno shrine (Otsu, Shiga).jpg|300px]] | Location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]] Shiga Prefecture Otsu City Wani‐naka 146 | Coordinates = {{WikiCoord2|35|09|21.689|N|135|54|58.078|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] | Sacred Object = | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = | Votive Boards, etc. = | Annual Festival = Second Sunday in May | Rituals = | Establishment = }}
'''Tenno Shrine''' (天皇神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Ōtsu|Otsu City]].
== Geography == [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]][[:en:Wani Station|Wani Station]] is located approximately 1.1 [[:en:Kilometre|kilometer]] to the southwest <ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/158|title=天皇神社{{!}}滋賀県観光情報|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわこビジターズビューロー|date=2015-06-23}}</ref>, and a 10‐minute walk away <ref name=":1">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://nishioumi.ct-net.com/00-05.shtml|title=歴史散歩・和邇天皇神社 和邇祭り|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=京都新聞西近江販売所連合|year=2000|month=05}}</ref>. It is situated along the roadside of Ryūka Crossing (present‐day {{ill|Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|ja|滋賀県道311号途中志賀線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture Route 311, Tokochu Shiga Line|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 311|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564859}}) {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. It is about 5 minutes from {{ill|Kosai Road|ja|湖西道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosai Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 琵琶湖西縦貫道路|1=Lake Biwa west running through road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11573837}} ([[:en:Japan National Route 161|National Route 161]]) {{ill|Wani IC|ja|和迩インターチェンジ|lt=Wani IC|lt_ja=和邇IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani IC|1=Wani interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418218}} <ref name=":2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://otsu.or.jp/thingstodo/spot213|title=天皇神社|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=びわ湖大津観光協会}}</ref>.
== History == In 966 ([[:en:Kōhō|Kōhō]] 3) the Gozu Tennō of [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was returned, and it is said that it began under the name "Wani Gozu Tennōsha" {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura]] <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in [[:en:1324|1324]] (the first year of [[:en:Shōchū (era)|Shōchū]]) and has been designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gabled Roof Construction|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Takamura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toad‐leg Motif|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|1=Minamihama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|1=Nakahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|1=Imaju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|1=Takashiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]]'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.
