In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.

The enshrinement is said to have been established in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura period}} <ref name=":0" />. In 1876 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 9) it acquired its current name {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. At that time, it became a village shrine <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=118|title=神社紹介 天皇神社（テンノウジンジャ）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=滋賀県神社庁}}</ref>.
== Shrine Grounds == The main hall was constructed in {{ill|1324|en|1324|WD=Q5773|lt=1324|1=1324}} (the first year of {{ill|Shōchū (era)|en|Shōchū (era)|WD=Q1327153|lt=Shōchū|1=Shōchū (era)}}) and has been designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} {{Sfn|滋賀県教育委員会事務局文化財保護課|2019|p=20}}. This main hall is one of only three examples of {{ill|Gabled Roof Construction|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gabled Roof Construction|1=Kirizuma-style|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}} in Shiga Prefecture (the others can be seen at {{ill|Ono Takamura Shrine|ja|小野篁神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Takamura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja|小野道風神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Dōfū Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) <ref name=":0" />. The format of having three main halls with a rear worship space in a gabled structure is rare nationwide <ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www5.city.otsu.shiga.jp/kankyou/content.asp?key=0401000000&skey=207|title=大津のかんきょう宝箱 天皇神社（和邇中）|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市環境部環境政策課}}</ref>. A characteristic feature from the time of its establishment is the remaining outline of a {{ill|Toad‐leg Motif|ja|蟇股|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toad‐leg Motif|1=kaerumata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123117911}} <ref name=":0" />. The roof is made of {{ill|Cypress Bark Roofing|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress Bark Roofing|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}} <ref name=":2" />.
== Annual Festival == The annual festival of Tenno Shrine, known as "Wani Festival" and sometimes referred to as "Goka Festival" <ref name=":1" />, is held by shrine officials from the Wani‐naka area and the districts of {{ill|Minamihama|ja|南浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamihama|1=Q11408105|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408105}}, {{ill|Nakahama|ja|中浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahama|1=Q16283273|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16283273}}, {{ill|Kitahama|ja|北浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitahama|1=Kitahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11402355}}, {{ill|Imaju|ja|今宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaju|1=Q11376872|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376872}}, and {{ill|Takashiro|ja|高城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashiro|1=Q11669044|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669044}} <ref name=":1" />. In the Minamihama district, on the early morning of the festival day, shrine officials depart from {{ill|Kimoto Shrine|ja|木元神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimoto Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to Tenno Shrine, and in the afternoon, portable shrines from other regions proceed to the lakeside resting area <ref name=":1" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/245.html|title=和邇祭{{!}}歴史事典|accessdate=2020-04-22|publisher=大津市歴史博物館}}</ref>. Its origin is attributed to the incident during {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}'s assault on {{ill|Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja|比叡山焼き討ち (1571st year )|lt=Mount Hiei Arson|lt_ja=比叡山焼き討ち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei Arson (1571)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, when the portable shrine of {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} fled to Tenno Shrine, which subsequently led to conflicts over its return <ref name=":1" />. It was once held annually on May 8, but nowadays, due to holiday scheduling, it is observed on the second Sunday of May each year <ref name=":1" />.
